code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about airports (or related entities) into a single,
    fluent English sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Simple mapping from predicate to a natural‑language fragment.
    # If a predicate is not in the map we fall back to a generic “subject predicate object” form.
    predicate_phrases = {
        "cityServed": "serves the city",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "is at an elevation of",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": "is at an elevation of",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": "is at an elevation of",
        "location": "is located in",
        "operatingOrganisation": "is operated by",
        "runwayLength": "has a runway length of",
        "runwayLengthMetre": "has a runway length of",
        "runwayLengthFeet": "has a runway length of",
        "runwayName": "has a runway named",
        "country": "is in the country",
        "isPartOf": "is part of",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": "has a first runway length of",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": "has a first runway length of",
        "1stRunwayNumber": "has a first runway numbered",
        "1stRunwaySurfaceType": "has a first runway surface of",
        "2ndRunwaySurfaceType": "has a second runway surface of",
        "3rdRunwayLengthFeet": "has a third runway length of",
        "3rdRunwaySurfaceType": "has a third runway surface of",
        "4thRunwayLengthFeet": "has a fourth runway length of",
        "4thRunwaySurfaceType": "has a fourth runway surface of",
        "5thRunwayNumber": "has a fifth runway numbered",
        "5thRunwaySurfaceType": "has a fifth runway surface of",
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": "has ICAO code",
        "locationIdentifier": "has location identifier",
        "iataLocationIdentifier": "has IATA code",
        "nativeName": "is also known as",
        "owner": "is owned by",
        "largestCity": "has the largest city",
        "capital": "has capital",
        "language": "speaks",
        "officialLanguage": "has official language",
        "leader": "is led by",
        "leaderParty": "belongs to the party",
        "leaderTitle": "holds the title",
        "mayor": "has mayor",
        "jurisdiction": "lies within jurisdiction of",
        "regionServed": "serves the region",
        "hubAirport": "uses hub airport",
        "aircraftFighter": "operates fighter aircraft",
        "aircraftHelicopter": "operates helicopter",
        "transportAircraft": "operates transport aircraft",
        "battle": "participated in",
        "currency": "uses currency",
        "headquarter": "has headquarters at",
        "foundingYear": "was founded in",
        "foundedBy": "was founded by",
        "postalCode": "has postal code",
        "areaCode": "has area code",
        "ceremonialCounty": "is in ceremonial county",
        "countySeat": "has county seat",
        "demonym": "is inhabited by",
        "class": "belongs to class",
        "division": "belongs to division",
        "order": "belongs to order",
        "administrativeArrondissement": "is in administrative arrondissement",
        "chief": "has chief",
        "runwaySurfaceType": "has runway surface of",
    }

    def phrase(triple: Triple) -> str:
        pred = triple.predicate
        subj = triple.subject
        obj = triple.object

        # Use the mapped phrase if available
        if pred in predicate_phrases:
            verb_phrase = predicate_phrases[pred]
            # Some predicates already imply the subject, e.g., "is at an elevation of"
            return f"{subj} {verb_phrase} {obj}"
        # Default fallback
        return f"{subj} {pred} {obj}"

    # Build a list of clause strings
    clauses = [phrase(t) for t in triples]

    # Combine clauses into one sentence
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        sentence = clauses[0] + "."
    else:
        # Join with commas, adding "and" before the last clause
        sentence = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1] + "."

    # Capitalize first character
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
